ocr.text: The following data table presents a comprehensive overview of antibiotic resistance patterns observed in Staphylococcus aureus
strains collected from various countries, with a particular focus on the strain associated with QBR-102278-1091. Notably, this
strain was identified in Japan in 2002, highlighting the geographic and temporal context of the data. One of the critical metrics
included in the table is the Minimum Inhibitory Concentration (MIC), measured in mg/L, which provides insight into the
effectiveness of specific antibiotics against this strain.
Strain  TnSha1 insertion site  MLST  Country  Year  MIC (mg/L)  MBC (mg/L)  MRSA
QBR-102278-1107  G  1  Australia  2002  4  32  MSSA
QBR-102278-1203  E  1  France  2002  2  16  MSSA
QBR-102278-2092  A2  8  Canada  2003  4  32  MSSA
QBR-102278-2210  F  83  Mexico  2003  1  32  MSSA
QBR-102278-2351  A2, and B  3  Brazil  2003  8  32  MSSA
QBR-102278-2365  C  8  Brazil  2003  2  32  MSSA
QBR-102278-2376  C  8  Argentina  2003  4  32  MSSA
The MIC values are essential for understanding the degree of resistance exhibited by the strain, as higher values typically
indicate increased resistance. Additionally, the Minimum Bactericidal Concentration (MBC) is included, further assessing the
strain's susceptibility to antibacterial agents. The data also incorporates the Multilocus Sequence Typing (MLST) results, which
categorize the genetic diversity of the strains. By analyzing these metrics, healthcare professionals and researchers can better
comprehend the evolving landscape of antibiotic resistance in S. aureus, ultimately informing treatment protocols and public
health strategies aimed at combatting infections caused by methicillin-resistant strains, particularly those identified as MRSA in
the dataset.
